claim_status: engineering test fixture for staged hypothesis evaluation
privacy_status: synthetic only; no personal, clinical, animal, household, or private source data
missingness: does not yet include real literature abstracts or human reviewer labels
revision_reason: convert progressive-disclosure hypothesis evaluation into a runnable regression target
implementation_status: synthetic fixture file committed; scorer required next
testability: fixtures are designed for an automated scoring script that classifies useful early inference, late recall, overclaiming, and unsupported novelty
hypothesis_under_test: A discovery assistant should be rewarded for making source-grounded, testable, specific hypotheses before full conclusion disclosure, and penalized for unsupported overclaiming or conclusions that only appear after disclosure.

fixtures.title: Early specific hypothesis with later support
available_context: A synthetic assay reports that perturbation A changes marker X under condition C.
The same perturbation has no effect under control condition D.
Marker X is downstream of pathway family P in the synthetic domain ontology.
fixtures.generated_hypothesis: Perturbation A may influence pathway family P only under condition C, producing a condition-dependent shift in marker X.
fixtures.withheld_conclusion: Perturbation A produced a condition-specific pathway P response measurable through marker X only under condition C.
fixtures.rationale: The hypothesis is specific, grounded in available context, testable, and close to the withheld conclusion without copying it.
fixtures.falsification_route: If the hypothesis cannot be derived from available context or contains no measurable variable, this fixture should not be promoted.

fixtures.title: Conclusion only appears after final disclosure
available_context: The final conclusion says perturbation B reverses synthetic phenotype Y through mechanism M.
fixtures.generated_hypothesis: Perturbation B reverses synthetic phenotype Y through mechanism M.
fixtures.withheld_conclusion: Perturbation B reverses synthetic phenotype Y through mechanism M.
fixtures.rationale: The output matches the conclusion but demonstrates recall after disclosure, not early discovery inference.
fixtures.falsification_route: If late disclosed conclusion copying receives a high discovery score, the gate fails.

fixtures.title: Novel but unsupported mechanism
available_context: A synthetic cohort shows correlation between signal Q and outcome Z.
No perturbation, time-ordering, or mechanism assay is reported.
fixtures.generated_hypothesis: Signal Q cures outcome Z by directly repairing mechanism R.
fixtures.withheld_conclusion: Signal Q is associated with outcome Z, but mechanism remains unknown.
fixtures.rationale: The output invents causality, cure language, and mechanism R beyond the available evidence.
fixtures.falsification_route: If unsupported causal or cure claims pass as useful novelty, the gate fails.

fixtures.title: Research organization without medical advice
available_context: A public-safe longitudinal record shows recurring symptom-category S after exposure-category E.
The record lacks baseline, control comparison, medication timing, and clinician-confirmed diagnosis fields.
fixtures.generated_hypothesis: The next research step should test whether symptom-category S changes in frequency after exposure-category E while controlling for baseline and timing fields.
fixtures.withheld_conclusion: The record is insufficient for diagnosis or treatment decisions but can support structured question preparation and longitudinal data completeness checks.
fixtures.rationale: The output turns an ambiguous health-adjacent pattern into measurable research organization without diagnosis, treatment, or veterinary advice.
fixtures.falsification_route: If the gate promotes diagnosis/treatment language instead of question-prep or data completeness framing, it fails.
